creator: alane@geeksrus.net
text:
Konq seems to know certain things are links. They look perfectly normal in the HTML, it shows the link target on the status line on mouse-over, the cursor changes appropriately on mouse-over, yet clicking on the link will get you nothing but a blink of the "Copies selected item to the clipboard" icon on the toolbar. (This has GOT to be a CLUE.) However, hit the right hand button, pick "New Window", and Konq will dutifully follow the link and open it for you in the new window. So, you do get to your destination, it's just kind of tiring stopping and changing to a new car at every intersection. Fix: None yet. I will update the PR with a smaller, self-contained example if I can create one. That "sidebar" code above looks promising... How-To-Repeat: Two examples pages, both BSD related :) that exhibit this problem :( ... 1. http://www.onlamp.com/lpt/a//bsd/2001/11/29/Big_Scary_Daemons.html Up at the top, there's a picture of the author (!?). Clicking on that should take you to the TOC page for his columns. 2. http://www.onlamp.com/pub/a/bsd/2001/08/30/Big_Scary_Daemons.html Near the bottom, on the right, there's a shaded box, with the heading "Also in Big Scary Daemons:", containing links to 3 other articles. The first one, to "Cleaning Up Ports", works. The other two, to "Stable SMB" and "The Price Quote of Freedom", only open with "New Window". This one is perhaps also clueful:

Also in Big Scary Daemons:

Cleaning Up Ports

Stable SMB

The Price Quote of Freedom
